headline: Four graphs to visualize the unprecedented scale of France's June heatwave
subtitle: France's most recent heatwave began on June 17 and reached unprecedented levels in terms of both peak temperatures and official alert status. It proved to be more intense than the infamous 2003 heatwave and could also surpass its duration.

Germany seeks to introduce a dose of Nordic-style 'flexicurity' into its labor law
subtitle: Through a series of reform plans, Berlin has committed to easing dismissal rules alongside measures to support job transitions, aiming to reduce the cost of failure for young companies.

headline: 250 years of American independence: 'Enslaved and freed Black people were not merely witnesses to the Atlantic Revolutions, they were active participants'
subtitle: Elisabeth Landi, a history professor, argues that commemorations marking the birth of the United States should fully acknowledge the memory of Black fighters who shed their blood for a revolution that did not challenge the institution of slavery.

headline: Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey' sparks a Homeric debate in Greece
subtitle: The casting of a Black actress as Helen of Troy has ignited fierce controversy in Greece. Some lament what they see as Hollywood's appropriation of Greek mythology, while others emphasize the universal nature of these ancient stories.

headline: Economist Gita Gopinath: 'Rising imbalances among major powers threaten growth and fuel protectionism'
subtitle: Harvard University economics professor and former International Monetary Fund official Gita Gopinath expresses concern over the level of US federal debt.

headline: Nearly 35,000 people became millionaires in France in 2025, UBS study finds
subtitle: The number of millionaires worldwide has reached record highs, according to the report. Real estate and finance remain the most effective ways to build wealth.

headline: 'We should not accept that heatwaves cause thousands of deaths. That is not normal'
